title: Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa
intro: Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa, also known as the few-veined oak, is a species of deciduous tree within the genus Cyclobalanopsis, native to southern China. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa is a species of evergreen tree in the family Fagaceae. This species belongs to the subgenus Cyclobalanopsis, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen oaks. It is native to China and is classified as an evergreen tree, with its leaves remaining green throughout the year. The exact classification of Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa is as a species of oak tree, specifically within the family Fagaceae. Further classification details are found within the family Fagaceae.
benefits: Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa is a species of deciduous tree native to China, and it is valued for its medicinal properties. The tree is believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help protect against chronic diseases. Traditional Chinese medicine has used the tree's bark and leaves to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa for medicinal purposes. Its potential health benefits may be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and polyphenols.
constituents: Cyclobalanopsis paucilamellosa is a species of plant that contains various active constituents. These constituents include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
